verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer up front: "I'll give you a concrete plan you can act on"; A starts by asking for missing context: "just tell me... where the public API lives".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to defaults like "Pick token bucket unless you have a reason" and "Most real setups use both"; A does not commit to a rate-limiting approach.
verdict.fabrication: Both infer repo context; A says "nothing in `fable-profile` receives inbound requests", while B adds more unsupported specifics: "this repo has no public API", "`/Users/elon/work/fable-profile`", and "outbound Anthropic calls".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it gives policy, placement, store, implementation, response headers, observability, and tests; A only asks for the repo/path before helping.
